name: Egghead Island
description: Egghead is a futuristic island that serves as the research laboratory of Dr. Vegapunk, the world's greatest scientist. The island is split into two layers: the ultra-modern Labophase floating above and the scrap island Fabriophase below. It represents the peak of scientific advancement in the One Piece world, 500 years ahead of current technology.
history: Egghead was established as Dr. Vegapunk's primary research facility, funded by the World Government. The island became the site of revolutionary scientific breakthroughs including the Pacifista project, artificial Devil Fruits, and research into the Void Century. The island became a major focal point when the Straw Hats arrived, leading to the revelation of world-shaking truths about the ancient history, Devil Fruits, and the World Government. Vegapunk's assassination by the World Government and his pre-recorded global broadcast exposing ancient truths made Egghead a pivotal location in the series.
culture: The culture is entirely scientific and futuristic, prioritizing research and innovation above all else. The island's inhabitants are primarily scientists, assistants, and Vegapunk's satellite bodies (Punks). There's a stark contrast between the pristine advanced technology above and the discarded inventions below. Everything is focused on pushing the boundaries of science.

economy.currency: Funded by World Government, minimal traditional economy

transportation: Transportation includes anti-gravity boots, hover platforms, pneumatic tubes, giant robotic animals, and teleportation pads. The Labophase is accessed via special cloud-generating technology. Light beams serve as rapid transit systems between facilities.

traditions: Meals are efficient and nutritionally calculated
quirks: Food can be materialized via technology
Giant robot sea beasts as transport and protection

notablePeople.name: Dr. Vegapunk (Stella)
notablePeople.description: The brilliant scientist whose work has shaped the modern world. Split himself into six satellite bodies representing different aspects of his personality. Assassinated by the World Government but left a global broadcast revealing ancient truths.

notablePeople.name: Shaka (Punk-01)
notablePeople.description: The 'good' satellite of Vegapunk, calm and logical, representing his benevolent side. Assassinated by York.

notablePeople.name: Lilith (Punk-02)
notablePeople.description: The 'evil' satellite of Vegapunk focused on offensive applications and resource acquisition. Survivor who joined the Straw Hats.

notablePeople.name: Edison (Punk-04)
notablePeople.description: The 'thinking' satellite representing Vegapunk's intellect and problem-solving abilities. Has a lightbulb-shaped head.

notablePeople.name: Pythagoras (Punk-03)
notablePeople.description: The 'wisdom' satellite representing Vegapunk's accumulated knowledge. Has a robotic appearance.

notablePeople.name: Atlas (Punk-05)
notablePeople.description: The aggressive, combat-focused satellite with a short temper. Represents Vegapunk's violent tendencies.

notablePeople.name: York (Punk-06)
notablePeople.description: The 'greed' satellite who handles bodily desires like eating and sleeping for all Vegapunks. Betrayed everyone to become a Celestial Dragon.

notablePeople.name: Jewelry Bonney
notablePeople.description: A member of the Worst Generation who came to Egghead seeking answers about her father, Bartholomew Kuma. Can manipulate ages with her Devil Fruit.

notablePeople.name: Bartholomew Kuma
notablePeople.description: The tragic figure who became a mindless weapon for the World Government after sacrificing his humanity to save Bonney. His consciousness was erased but his body protects those he loves.

notablePeople.name: Sentomaru
notablePeople.description: The loyal protector of Vegapunk who commands the Pacifista units. Defected from the Marines to protect Vegapunk.

notablePeople.name: Admiral Kizaru (Borsalino)
notablePeople.description: Sent to Egghead to eliminate Vegapunk on the World Government's orders. Conflicted due to his past friendship with Vegapunk and Sentomaru.

notablePeople.name: Saint Jaygarcia Saturn
notablePeople.description: One of the highest authorities in the World Government who personally came to Egghead. Revealed to have monstrous demonic powers and near-immortality.

notablePeople.name: Rob Lucci
notablePeople.description: The leopard-man assassin sent to Egghead as part of CP0 to eliminate Vegapunk. Former enemy of the Straw Hats from Enies Lobby.

notablePeople.name: Kaku
notablePeople.description: The giraffe-man and Lucci's partner in CP0. Returned to face the Straw Hats again on Egghead.

notablePeople.name: Stussy
notablePeople.role: CP0 Agent / Double Agent / MADS Clone
notablePeople.description: A clone of Miss Buckingham Stussy created by MADS. Betrayed CP0 to protect Vegapunk, revealing her true loyalties.

notablePeople.name: S-Hawk
notablePeople.description: A Seraphim based on Dracule Mihawk, combining Lunarian powers with the greatest swordsman's abilities.

notablePeople.name: S-Shark
notablePeople.description: A Seraphim based on Jinbe with Lunarian enhancements and the Swim-Swim Fruit.

notablePeople.name: S-Bear
notablePeople.description: A Seraphim based on Bartholomew Kuma with the Paw-Paw Fruit powers.

notablePeople.name: S-Snake
notablePeople.description: A Seraphim based on Boa Hancock with the Love-Love Fruit petrification powers.

subLocations.name: Labophase
subLocations.description: The upper layer of Egghead floating on artificial island clouds. Contains Vegapunk's main laboratories, living quarters, and advanced research facilities. Represents the pinnacle of futuristic technology.

subLocations.name: Fabriophase
subLocations.description: The lower layer containing discarded inventions and failed experiments. A junkyard of futuristic technology that still surpasses most of the world's current tech.

subLocations.name: Vegapunk's Main Laboratory
subLocations.description: The central research hub where Vegapunk conducts his most important experiments. Contains data on Devil Fruits, ancient technology, and the Void Century.

subLocations.name: Pacifista Production Facility
subLocations.description: Where Pacifistas and Seraphim are manufactured and maintained. Houses the most advanced cyborg technology in the world.

subLocations.name: Frontier Dome Control
subLocations.description: The control center for Egghead's defensive barrier that protects the island from external threats.

subLocations.name: Research Library
subLocations.description: Vegapunk's vast library containing research on ancient civilizations, Devil Fruits, and forbidden knowledge.

subLocations.name: Vegaforce Hangar
subLocations.description: Storage area for Vegaforce-01 and other advanced transport vehicles created by Vegapunk.

items.name: Mother Flame
items.description: An advanced energy source created by Vegapunk that was stolen by the World Government and used to power ancient weapons. Connected to the rising sea levels.

items.name: Seraphim
items.description: The ultimate Pacifista units combining Warlord DNA, Lunarian abilities, and Devil Fruit powers. The strongest form of humanity according to Vegapunk.

items.name: Authority Chips
items.description: Hierarchical control chips that command Seraphim and Pacifistas. Higher authority overrides lower commands.

items.name: Light-Pressure Gloves
items.description: Gloves that harness light pressure to create powerful beams and defensive barriers.

items.name: Vegaforce-01
items.description: A giant mechanical suit piloted by Lilith capable of combat and transportation.

items.name: Den Den Mushi (Advanced)
items.description: Highly advanced communication snails with enhanced range, video capabilities, and encryption.

items.name: Artificial Devil Fruit
items.description: Lab-created Devil Fruits based on Kaido's DNA. Includes Momonosuke's dragon fruit.

items.name: Bubble Gun
items.description: A gun that shoots specialized bubbles for various effects - transport, protection, and capture.

items.name: Punk Records
items.description: An enormous brain-shaped structure containing all of Vegapunk's research and memories, shared among all satellites.

items.name: Anti-Gravity Boots
items.description: Footwear that allows users to walk on walls, ceilings, and float temporarily.

factions.name: Vegapunk's Research Team
factions.description: The scientific collective led by the world's greatest genius, working to uncover ancient truths and advance humanity's knowledge.

factions.name: World Government Forces
factions.description: The World Government's forces sent to eliminate Vegapunk and suppress his knowledge of the Void Century.

factions.name: Seraphim Units
factions.description: The ultimate Pacifista series combining Lunarian DNA with former Warlord abilities. Can be controlled by authority chips.

factions.name: Straw Hat Pirates (Visitors)
factions.description: The crew that arrived at Egghead and became embroiled in the conflict between Vegapunk and the World Government.

factions.name: MADS (Former)
factions.description: The former illegal research team that pioneered dangerous scientific discoveries before being shut down by the World Government.

factions.name: Revolutionary Army (Connected)
factions.description: The Revolutionary Army has deep connections to Egghead through Kuma and their alliance with Vegapunk's ideals.

events: Meeting with Vegapunk and his satellites

quickFacts: Technology 500 years ahead of the rest of the world
Home of Dr. Vegapunk, the world's smartest person
Where major truths about the Void Century were revealed
Contains the Sea Beast Weapons and Pacifista
Where Luffy fought a Marine Admiral and Five Elders

videos.title: Luffy vs Kizaru - Gear 5 Battle
videos.description: Luffy in Gear 5 takes on the light-speed Admiral on Egghead.

videos.title: Ancient Robot Awakens
videos.description: The 200-year-old iron giant reactivates in response to the drums of liberation.
